title: Interactively Generating Explanations for Transformer Language Models

structured.summary: This paper proposes Proto-Trex, a prototype-based transformer language model that integrates a prototype layer for inherently interpretable, case-based explanations, and iProto-Trex, an interactive framework that allows users to revise prototype explanations through a novel, gradient-free 'plug & play' interaction. Experiments on sentiment classification datasets demonstrate comparable accuracy to baseline transformers while enabling user-guided explanation refinement without degrading performance.

strengths.point: Integration of prototype layer within transformer LMs for inherent interpretability
strengths.evidence: Proto-Trex provides explanations by identifying prototypical examples from training data, improving transparency over black-box transformers.

strengths.point: Interactive explanation refinement without gradient tracing
strengths.evidence: iProto-Trex enables users to revise prototype explanations via 'soft replacing' with certainty weights, forming a human-in-the-loop cooperative learning loop.

strengths.point: Empirical validation showing explanation refinement without loss of accuracy
strengths.evidence: Experiments on Yelp and Movie Review datasets demonstrate that interactive prototype edits improve explanation quality while maintaining classification accuracy.

weaknesses.point: Lack of detailed comparison to prior prototype-based methods and post-hoc explanation baselines
weaknesses.evidence: No structured side-by-side analysis with seminal prototype models (e.g., ProtoPNet) or saliency methods like Integrated Gradients, LIME, or SHAP is provided.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient ablation studies and unclear prototype design choices
weaknesses.evidence: No experiments isolating the impact of number of prototypes, embedding dimensionality, or similarity metrics on performance and explanation quality.

weaknesses.point: Opaque description of the interactive prototype revision mechanism
weaknesses.evidence: Algorithmic details or pseudocode for the 'soft replacing' interaction and handling of conflicting user edits are missing, limiting reproducibility.

weaknesses.point: Evaluation metrics focus narrowly on accuracy, neglecting explanation faithfulness and user trust
weaknesses.evidence: No established explanation quality metrics (e.g., deletion/insertion tests) or user study details (participant info, qualitative feedback) are reported.

weaknesses.point: Limited task scope and scalability discussion
weaknesses.evidence: Experiments are restricted to binary sentiment classification on small datasets and models, with no analysis of scalability to larger transformers or other NLP tasks.

weaknesses.point: Reproducibility hampered by missing training details and environment specifications
weaknesses.evidence: Critical aspects such as prototype initialization, update rules, hyperparameters, software versions, and hardware used are not clearly described.

weaknesses.point: Unclear how user edits affect model parameters vs. explanation representations
weaknesses.evidence: The paper does not clarify whether interactive edits update model weights, prototype embeddings, or only explanation outputs.

weaknesses.point: No analysis of prototype representativeness, diversity, or error cases
weaknesses.evidence: There is no investigation into whether prototypes comprehensively cover data distribution or help diagnose model misclassifications.

questions.question: How does Proto-Trex compare quantitatively and qualitatively to established prototype learning models and post-hoc explanation techniques?
questions.motivation: Understanding relative gains in interpretability and performance is crucial to assess novelty and practical advantage.

questions.question: What is the number and frequency of user interactions needed for effective explanation refinement, and how scalable is the interactive process?
questions.motivation: Evaluating user effort and interaction efficiency impacts usability and adoption in real applications.

questions.question: Does the prototype layer impact model robustness or generalization to out-of-distribution inputs?
questions.motivation: Assessing robustness is important as interpretability methods can interact with model reliability.

questions.question: How are conflicting user edits handled during interactive prototype revision, especially with multiple users or uncertain feedback?
questions.motivation: Clarifying this affects the safety and stability of human-in-the-loop learning.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of detailed comparison to prior prototype-based methods and post-hoc explanation baselines. No structured side-by-side analysis with seminal prototype models (e.g., ProtoPNet) or saliency methods like Integrated Gradients, LIME, or SHAP is provided
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient ablation studies and unclear prototype design choices. No experiments isolating the impact of number of prototypes, embedding dimensionality, or similarity metrics on performance and explanation quality
critique_points.point_003: Opaque description of the interactive prototype revision mechanism. Algorithmic details or pseudocode for the 'soft replacing' interaction and handling of conflicting user edits are missing, limiting reproducibility
critique_points.point_004: Evaluation metrics focus narrowly on accuracy, neglecting explanation faithfulness and user trust. No established explanation quality metrics (e.g., deletion/insertion tests) or user study details (participant info, qualitative feedback) are reported
critique_points.point_005: Limited task scope and scalability discussion. Experiments are restricted to binary sentiment classification on small datasets and models, with no analysis of scalability to larger transformers or other NLP tasks
critique_points.point_006: Reproducibility hampered by missing training details and environment specifications. Critical aspects such as prototype initialization, update rules, hyperparameters, software versions, and hardware used are not clearly described
critique_points.point_007: Unclear how user edits affect model parameters vs. explanation representations. The paper does not clarify whether interactive edits update model weights, prototype embeddings, or only explanation outputs
critique_points.point_008: No analysis of prototype representativeness, diversity, or error cases. There is no investigation into whether prototypes comprehensively cover data distribution or help diagnose model misclassifications
